title: Rabdosia × togashii
intro: Rabdosia × togashii, also known as Togashii's Rabdosia or Japanese Rabdosia, is a hybrid plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rabdosia × togashii include diterpenoid compounds like 19- nor- labdane diterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rabdosia × togashii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or in the form of a tincture or extract, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Rabdosia × togashii is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is known to be a cross between Rabdosia rubescens and another species within the same genus. The exact classification of the second species involved in the hybridization is not widely documented. As a result, the full classification of Rabdosia × togashii is not explicitly stated in most botanical resources. Further research is needed to clarify its exact classification and relationships within the Lamiaceae family.

Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a subject of interest in the development of new antimicrobial agents.
